text: Referendums: From Rigged To Raw Covers a whole range of referendums held across the world since 1793 (an enormous survey has been conducted for the purpose of this book). For the first time in the historiography of direct democracy it is possible to give a full and academically informed account of what has triggered referendums through time, and why and when policies and constitutional issues have been submitted to the peoples’ veto. Thus Referendums Around the World examines from a historical as well as political perspective the role that referendums may have played over time. To provide a definitive account of what has been happening across the world over time is, by any standards, vital to any understanding of citizens being allowed to make decisions through the democratic ballot box, and thereby a study of referendums enables us to examine an understanding of popular democracy.
explanation: The passage reads like a conventional book description with somewhat verbose marketing language; there are no strong AI-specific artifacts, so human authorship remains plausible but not certain.
